title: Hieracium heterogynum
taxonomy: Hieracium heterogynum is a species of plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It is commonly found in mountainous regions and belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is often classified as a perennial herb, and its classification is further divided into the subgenus Hieracium and the section Hieracium. Within the plant kingdom, Hieracium heterogynum is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales. Further classification breaks it down into the family Asteraceae, subfamily Cichorioideae, and tribe Cichorieae.

properties: Hieracium heterogynum, also known as yellow hawkweed, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its therapeutic properties are said to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it potentially useful for wound healing and reducing infection risk. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, yellow hawkweed has been used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a diuretic. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Hieracium heterogynum for medicinal use.
constituents: Hieracium heterogynum is known to contain sesquiterpene lactones, which are a group of compounds found in various plants of the Asteraceae family. These lactones are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal properties. The specific sesquiterpene lactones present in Hieracium heterogynum include heterogynoside and heterogynoside acetate. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of Hieracium heterogynum's active constituents.

intro: Hieracium heterogynum, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium heterogynum include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Hawkweed include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
